name: Tai Dam

ancestors.notes: Descent per Glottolog classification of Tai Dam (taid1247).

notes: Tai (Southwestern Tai) language of northwestern Vietnam and neighbouring areas, written with the Tai Viet script. Tai Viet is an abugida with paired high- and low-class consonant symbols, an inherent vowel, separately positioned vowel signs and only partial, often optional tone marking, so a consonant symbol's reading depends on its class and on tone that the spelling frequently does not record. Converting a spelling to IPA requires a per-word pronunciation lexicon that the engine does not bundle; grapheme->IPA coverage is lexicon-dependent and effectively zero without that stratum, and no letter->IPA table is asserted. Tai Dam is tonal, and tone is left unencoded.

sources.author: Hammarström, H. et al. (Glottolog 5.0)
sources.title: Glottolog: Tai Dam (taid1247)
